title: Traumatic Brain Injury (TBI)
metaTitle: Traumatic Brain Injury (TBI) — Accident Causes & Legal Rights
metaDescription: Traumatic brain injuries from accidents range from concussion to severe TBI. Learn symptoms, long-term effects, and your rights in CA and AZ.
description: Traumatic brain injuries range from mild concussions to severe, life-altering brain damage. TBI can occur without any visible head wound — rapid acceleration and deceleration alone can cause serious injury. TBI is one of the most complex and costly accident injuries, with effects that may not fully appear for days or weeks.
symptoms: Headache or pressure in the head
Memory loss — before, during, or after the injury
Sleep disturbances — difficulty sleeping or sleeping excessively
Mood changes, irritability, anxiety, or depression
longTermEffects: Post-concussion syndrome — symptoms persisting weeks or months
Chronic traumatic encephalopathy (CTE) from repeated brain trauma
Cognitive impairment affecting memory, attention, and executive function
Increased risk of Alzheimer's disease and other neurodegenerative conditions
Permanent physical disability in severe cases
Inability to return to work or maintain prior level of function
treatmentOptions: Immediate emergency stabilization for severe TBI
CT scan and MRI imaging to assess injury extent
Cognitive rest and graduated return to activity for mild TBI
Medication for symptoms including headache, nausea, seizures, and mood
Speech and language therapy for communication impairments
Occupational therapy for daily function recovery
Psychological counseling for mood and emotional effects
commonCauses: Car, truck, and motorcycle accidents (leading cause of TBI in adults)
Slip and fall accidents, especially head impacts
Construction site accidents from falls or struck-by objects
Workplace accidents involving blunt force or falls
